articles.title: Rochdale Friends Complete 1,500km Arctic Ascent Cycling Challenge
articles.excerpt: A group of friends from Rochdale has raised over £122,000 for Springhill Hospice by completing a 1,500km cycling challenge in the Arctic.
articles.content_html:
A group of friends from Rochdale has completed a 1,500km cycling challenge called the Arctic Ascent. The event raised more than £122,000 for Springhill Hospice.

The cyclists undertook the challenge to support the hospice, which provides care for patients with life-limiting illnesses. Their efforts have significantly contributed to the charity's funding.

The team faced various challenges during their journey, including harsh weather conditions and difficult terrain. Despite these obstacles, they remained committed to their goal of raising funds for the hospice.

The funds raised will help Springhill Hospice continue its vital work in the community, providing support and care to those in need.

articles.title: Rochdale's Major New Scheme Aims to Enhance Local Neighbourhoods
articles.excerpt: A new scheme in Rochdale aims to provide a well-designed neighbourhood for residents. The project is expected to enhance the local area significantly. Details about the specific features of the development have not yet been disclosed, but it is anticipated to include various housing options.
articles.content_html:
A new scheme in Rochdale aims to provide a well-designed neighbourhood for residents. The project is expected to enhance the local area significantly.

Details about the specific features of the development have not yet been disclosed, but it is anticipated to include various housing options.

Local authorities have expressed their commitment to improving community spaces and infrastructure as part of this initiative.

Further updates on the project's progress will be provided as they become available.

articles.title: Rochdale Launches Drug and Alcohol Diversity Programme
articles.excerpt: A new programme aimed at addressing drug and alcohol issues in Rochdale has been launched by Action Together.
articles.content_html:
A new programme aimed at addressing drug and alcohol issues in Rochdale has been launched by Action Together. The initiative promotes diversity and inclusivity in support services for individuals affected by substance misuse.

The Rochdale Drug and Alcohol Diversity Programme will provide tailored support to meet the varied needs of the community. It aims to engage with different cultural groups to ensure that services are accessible and effective.

Action Together stated that the programme will involve collaboration with local organisations and stakeholders to enhance the support network available to residents.

The launch of the programme is likely to improve access to vital resources for those struggling with addiction in Rochdale.

articles.title: Calls for Crackdown on HMOs in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Concerns have been raised regarding the proliferation of Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) in Rochdale, prompting calls for stricter regulations.
articles.content_html:
Concerns have been raised about the increasing number of Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) in Rochdale. Local residents have expressed worries regarding the impact of these properties on the community.

Calls for a crackdown on HMOs have intensified, with some residents advocating for stricter regulations to manage their growth. They argue that the rise in HMOs has led to issues such as overcrowding.

Local councillors are reportedly discussing potential measures to address these concerns, including reviewing planning policies related to HMOs. The aim is to balance affordable housing with community standards.

Further discussions are expected in upcoming council meetings as residents continue to voice their concerns.

articles.title: Rochdale Masonic Hall Celebrates Centenary
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Masonic Hall marks its centenary with a special ceremony, including a re-enactment of the foundation stone laying.
articles.content_html:
A ceremony to celebrate the centenary of Rochdale Masonic Hall took place recently, featuring a re-enactment of the laying of the foundation stone.

The event was organised by the Provincial Grand Lodge of East Lancashire, highlighting the historical significance of the hall in the community.

Attendees were able to witness the traditional ceremony, which included an explanation of its importance and the rituals involved.

The centenary celebration reflects the hall's long-standing presence and role within Rochdale's social fabric.

articles.title: New Rock Choir Group Launches in Rochdale with Free Taster Sessions
articles.excerpt: A new Rock Choir group has launched in Rochdale, offering free taster sessions for those interested in joining.
articles.content_html:
A new Rock Choir group has launched in Rochdale, providing free taster sessions for anyone interested in joining. The sessions aim to encourage community participation and allow potential members to experience the choir's activities before committing to membership.

Participants can expect a welcoming environment where they can explore singing in a group setting. The choir is designed for people of all abilities, making it accessible to anyone who enjoys music.

The free taster sessions will take place at various times, allowing flexibility for those interested. Further details about the schedule and how to join can be obtained from the choir's official channels.

This initiative is part of a broader effort to enhance community engagement through music and the arts in Rochdale.

articles.title: Missing Cat Gene Last Seen in Falinge Area of Rochdale
articles.excerpt: A grey and white cat named Gene has gone missing from the Falinge area of Rochdale. He is microchipped but not wearing a collar.
articles.content_html:
A cat named Gene has been reported missing from the Falinge area of Rochdale. The grey and white cat was last seen a couple of days ago.

Gene is microchipped but is not wearing a collar. He is known to be an outdoor cat, often coming and going as he pleases.

His owner has expressed concern, noting that it is unusual for him not to return home. Anyone who may have seen Gene or has information about his whereabouts is encouraged to reach out.

The missing cat's last known location was near Wasilla Drive and Clark Street in Rochdale. Residents in the area are asked to keep an eye out for him.

articles.title: 194kg of Produce Transferred to Rochdale Food Bank Following Pantry Closure
articles.excerpt: Following the closure of Irene’s Pantry, 194kg of produce was transferred to Rochdale Food Bank by local residents. The community effort was acknowledged at St James.
articles.content_html:
A group of residents transferred 194kg of produce to Rochdale Food Bank following the closure of Irene’s Pantry. The donation was made by Anne, Peter and Walter, who facilitated the transfer.

The food was hosted at St James, where the community effort was appreciated. Those involved in the operation of the Food Bank were thanked for their contributions.

This initiative highlights the ongoing support for local food banks, which play a crucial role in assisting those in need within the community.

The closure of Irene’s Pantry has prompted local residents to step in and ensure that surplus food is redirected to those who require it.
